name: AnyCrawl
description: AnyCrawl 🚀: A Node.js/TypeScript crawler that turns websites into LLM-ready data and extracts structured SERP results from Google/Bing/Baidu/etc. Native multi-threading for bulk processing.

new version v0.0.1-beta.19

commit.message:
✨ feat: add persistent proxy cache and harden page cache validation

- add Redis-backed `ProxyCacheManager` to persist domain-level proxy mode decisions and proxy-level failure cooldown entries
- wire proxy cache into proxy selection, engine error handling, and request success recording so working proxies are reused and `auto` mode can upgrade to stealth based on domain history
- tighten page cache quality gates by rejecting empty/title-only extraction payloads in `shouldCache` and re-validating cached S3 payloads before returning cache hits
- add `shouldCache` tests for title-only markdown rejection plus valid markdown and screenshot-only passthrough cases
- keep session pool enabled while dropping the explicit `persistCookiesPerSession` default override in engine factory defaults
📝 docs: align general docs with current API behavior
✨ feat: add postgres docker compose stack

✨ feat: add cloudflare challenge orchestration with 2captcha solver

- add challenge plugin infrastructure (`ChallengeContext`, `ChallengeOrchestrator`, `ChallengePlugin`) and a Cloudflare handler that detects challenges, tracks solve state, and requests stealth upgrade/proxy rotation when needed
- implement a full Turnstile solving stack (`CDPTurnstileSolver`, provider interface, `TwoCaptchaTurnstileProvider`, `TwoCaptchaTurnstileClient`) with runtime interception, multi-source param extraction, token injection, and retry/timeout guards
- wire challenge flow into engine lifecycle (`EngineConfigurator` + `Base`) with pre/post navigation hooks, post-challenge settle/reload status handling, retry markers for errorHandler, and payload `challenge` enrichment
- update scrape/proxy/cache behavior by defaulting `proxy` to `auto`, using proxy-mode-aware wait timeouts, escalating auto retries to stealth tier, and preserving original proxy mode in cache key computation
- add new stealth/2captcha env settings and relax HTMLTransformer exclusions to avoid dropping primary content containers under `only_main_content`

🔧 fix: make markdown OCR concurrency configurable and align OCR docs

- read OCR concurrency from ANYCRAWL_OCR_CONCURRENCY (default: 5, min: 1)
- add ANYCRAWL_OCR_CONCURRENCY=5 to .env.example
- document ocr_options as markdown-only in scrape/crawl/search/mcp docs
- stop tracking docs/json-options.md to match .gitignore

v1.0.0-beta.13

commit.message:
✨ feat: add OCR-enhanced markdown extraction with configurable vision provider

- add `LLMOCR` agent based on OpenAI-compatible provider and expose OCR exports/types
- add `ocr_options` to scrape/crawl/search schemas, queue options, controllers, and cache key composition
- enhance markdown extraction to OCR image content and inject `[ANYCRAWL_OCR_TEXT]...[/ANYCRAWL_OCR_TEXT]` blocks after image tokens
- add `MarkdownOCR` utility and unit tests for image parsing, OCR block injection, and text normalization
- add OCR-related env vars in `.env.example`

✨ feat: introduce idempotent billing ledger and unify billing/scheduled-task/webhook flows

- add `billing_ledger` with idempotency keys and `charge_details`, supporting both `delta` and `target` billing modes
- integrate Billing + `CreditCalculator` charge details across middleware, scheduler, scrape engines, and API controllers
- add `OwnerAccess` utilities for consistent ownership checks in DB queries
- add scheduled-task webhook association module and support PostgreSQL/SQLite JSON-specific association queries
- refactor `ScheduledTasksController`/`WebhooksController` with shared schemas and pagination handling
- refactor `CrawlController`/`MapController`/`ScrapeController`/`SearchController` to use `billingChargeDetails` with consistent `creditsUsed`
- enhance AI extraction flow with schema validation, empty-input handling, and default cost fallback in `BaseAgent`
- add billing integration/unit tests (idempotency, retry logic, charge details, error paths) and update DB migrations/scripts
- update `.gitignore` to exclude `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md`
🔧 fix: only check credits for billing endpoints and update baseline-browser-mapping
release: v1.0.0-beta.15
fix: add axios dependency for scrape webhook
docs: update changelog for v1.0.0-beta.13
🐛 fix: correct cache key params and consolidate desc import from @anycrawl/db

✨ feat: add page cache and map cache with S3 storage support

- Add CacheManager for page cache (S3-backed) and map cache (DB-backed)
- Support max_age, store_in_cache, use_index parameters in scrape/crawl/map APIs
- Add page_cache and map_cache database tables (PostgreSQL/SQLite)
- Track cache hits per job with cache_hits column
- Add cache documentation and update OpenAPI spec
- Update JS-SDK types for cache parameters

🔧 chore: clean up unused code and fix search credits calculation

- Remove unused SEARCH_CANCELLED and MAP_CANCELLED webhook event types
- Fix search credits calculation by passing pages parameter to CreditCalculator
- Remove unused @anycrawl/search dependency from scrape package

📝 docs: update CHANGELOG with releases from beta.9 to beta.12

- Add 1.0.0-beta.12: proxy modes, session rotation, MCP guides
- Add 1.0.0-beta.11: template task type support
- Add 1.0.0-beta.10: webhook/scheduler managers, IP whitelisting
- Add 1.0.0-beta.9: scrape_paths parameter for selective extraction

✨ feat: add comprehensive webhook events and enhance scheduled task execution tracking

- Add webhook events for scrape, crawl, search, and map operations (created/started/completed/failed/cancelled)
- Add cancel execution endpoint for scheduled tasks (DELETE /v1/scheduled-tasks/:taskId/executions/:executionId)
- Refactor task_executions schema to retrieve metrics via JOIN from jobs table instead of duplicating data
- Enhance execution history response with job metrics (credits_used, items_processed, duration_ms, etc.)
- Add execution status tracking in Worker with started_at/completed_at timestamps
- Support credit deduction for scheduled task scrape jobs
- Update docs with new cancel execution API and enhanced execution response fields
🔧 chore: replace internal extractUrlsFromCheerio with crawlee implementation

git commit -m "✨ feat: add map API support to JS-SDK

- Add MapLink, MapRequest, and MapResult types to SDK types
- Implement map() method for discovering all URLs from a website
- Integrate map method into AnyCrawlClient class
- Update README with map usage examples and API documentation
- Add map to API surface list"

✨ feat: add links format for extracting all URLs from scraped pages

- Add 'links' to SCRAPE_FORMATS constant in libs/constants.ts
- Implement link extraction using crawlee's extractUrlsFromCheerio in DataExtractor
- Update SDK types to include links?: string[] in ScrapeResultSuccess
- Update API documentation for scrape, crawl, and search endpoints
- Add detailed links format description with features and use cases
chore: add auto-claude entries to .gitignore
✨ feat: simplify map endpoint by making search engine discovery default behavior
✨ feat: add map endpoint for website URL discovery and extraction
✨ feat: add summary as a new output format for web content summarization
✨ feat: enhance HTML to Markdown with lazy-loaded images and section element support

✨ feat: enhance HTML to Markdown conversion with GFM support and smart content extraction

- Add turndown-plugin-gfm for GitHub Flavored Markdown (tables, strikethrough, task lists)
- Implement comprehensive test suite for html-to-markdown with 50+ test cases
- Add smart fallback mechanism when main content extraction yields minimal results
- Introduce only_main_content option to control content filtering (default: true)
- Enhance code block extraction with language detection and line number removal
- Improve link and image handling with better whitespace normalization
- Add performance monitoring with detailed metrics logging
- Implement force-include logic to preserve main content within excluded containers
- Separate ALWAYS_REMOVE_TAGS (script/style) from conditional EXCLUDE_NON_MAIN_TAGS
- Add post-processing for multiline links, skip-to-content removal, and image spacing

✨ feat: add subscription tiers with scheduled tasks limits and enhanced credit estimation

- Add subscription tier support to database schemas (PostgreSQL & SQLite)
- Implement scheduled tasks limits based on subscription tier (free/paid)
- Add credit estimation for tasks before execution
- Create webhook helper utility for centralized webhook handling
- Extract proxy functionality into dedicated module
- Update scheduled tasks documentation with tier-based limits
- Enhance credit calculation with task estimation support

commit.message: 🐛 fix(docs): handle missing page description in metadata generation
📝 docs: add MCP setup guides and improve SEO with structured data

✨ feat: add proxy modes (auto/base/stealth) with centralized credit calculation

- Introduce proxy mode system: auto, base, stealth, or custom URL
- Add ANYCRAWL_PROXY_STEALTH_URL for premium/residential proxies
- Create CreditCalculator class for unified credit calculation
- Add stealth proxy credits via ANYCRAWL_PROXY_STEALTH_CREDITS
- Refactor ScrapeController, CrawlController, SearchController to use CreditCalculator
- Update Progress manager to use centralized credit calculation
- Add proxy mode resolution with tier-based fallback support
- Include resolved proxy mode in scrape responses
- Update documentation with proxy modes usage examples
feat: enhance development scripts and improve queue handling in Worker, add detailed logging for Webhook and Scheduler managers
feat: add session options to rotate after every error in engine configurator

feat: enhance scheduled tasks with credit estimation and validation

- Added new credit estimation function to calculate minimum credits required for tasks.
- Updated PostgreSQL and SQLite schemas to set default minCreditsRequired to 1.
- Introduced credit checks in SchedulerManager to auto-pause tasks with insufficient credits.
- Improved error handling and logging for credit checks.
- Added method to check if the scheduler is running.
- Refactored task removal logic to use jobId pattern for efficiency.
fix: update task schema to allow nullable description and max executions per day

✨ feat: add webhook and scheduler managers with support for scheduled tasks and webhook events

- Introduced SchedulerManager for handling scheduled tasks using BullMQ repeatable jobs.
- Implemented WebhookManager for managing webhook subscriptions and deliveries.
- Enhanced Job model to include user identification in job requests.
- Added new types for webhook events and payload structures.
- Updated package.json files to include necessary dependencies for cron and webhook functionalities.

commit.message:
✨ feat: add network traffic tracking for jobs

- Introduced new fields in the jobs schema for tracking network traffic usage: trafficBytes, trafficRequestBytes, trafficResponseBytes, and trafficRequestCount.
- Implemented addTraffic method in Job model to increment traffic metrics atomically.
- Created RequestMetric type definitions for structured traffic data.
- Developed BandwidthManager to handle traffic metrics recording and flushing to the database.
- Integrated bandwidth tracking into the EngineConfigurator and BaseEngine classes.
- Enhanced progress management to flush bandwidth data upon job completion.
- Added BandwidthTracker to manage CDP-based traffic tracking for browser engines.

✨ feat(Auth): implement IP address whitelisting for API keys, enhancing security by allowing only specified IPs to access the API. Update logging middleware to capture client IPs and add utility functions for IP validation. Fixed IP retrive error.

✨ feat(proxy): enhance proxy handling by introducing original_url for improved rule matching and logging, allowing better tracking of requests and session management during retries, particularly for 403 errors.

commit.message: ✨ feat(docker): update Dockerfiles to include template-client package and adjust build commands for improved dependency management and clarity across multiple Dockerfiles.
✨ docs(docker): update documentation to format container name placeholder with backticks for improved clarity and readability.
✨ feat(http-client): redefine HttpResponse interface to prevent circular dependency during build, enhancing type safety and clarity in HTTP response handling.
✨ refactor(http-client): replace local HttpResponse interface with re-export from @anycrawl/scrape, streamlining type management and reducing build complexity.
✨ chore(template-client): add optional peer dependency for @anycrawl/scrape in package.json and update http-client to handle dynamic import, improving flexibility in dependency management.
✨ chore(template-client): remove unnecessary peerDependenciesMeta for @anycrawl/scrape in package.json, simplifying dependency management.
✨ feat(http-client): re-export HttpResponse type to prevent circular dependency during build, enhancing type safety and clarity in HTTP response handling.
✨ feat(docker): further optimize Dockerfile build process by separating build commands for base libraries and remaining packages, enhancing clarity and performance in the build workflow.

✨ feat(docker): add TypeScript and ESLint config files to Dockerfile, ensuring necessary configurations are included for the build process; streamline build commands for improved clarity and efficiency.
✨ feat(docker): split build commands in Dockerfile for better clarity and performance, separating the build process for different packages to optimize the build workflow.
✨ feat(docker): add template-client package to Dockerfile, including its dependencies and build process, ensuring proper integration into the application.

✨ feat(api): implement a new API key generation feature with a dedicated command and script; enhance documentation for generating API keys in both local and Docker environments, ensuring users can easily authenticate their requests.
✨ feat(env): add new environment variables for template execution and request handler timeouts; update Base and EngineFactory to utilize these variables for improved configuration flexibility.

✨ feat(template): introduce template validation and execution enhancements, including a trusted flag for templates, improved security measures in code execution, and new HTTP client integration for template-based requests; update related schemas and validation logic to support these features, ensuring better handling of user-defined templates.

✨ feat(docs): add `extract_source` field to OpenAPI schema and documentation, allowing users to specify the source format for JSON extraction (html or markdown); update related documentation to reflect this new option.

✨ feat(api): implement template field validation in Crawl, Scrape, and Search controllers to ensure only allowed fields are used when a template_id is provided, enhancing request data integrity and security.

✨ feat(html-to-markdown): enhance markdown conversion by adding rules for linked images, figure/picture normalization, and figcaption handling; implement post-processing to clean up bracketed images and whitespace for improved output quality.

✨ feat(docs): add OpenAPI template documentation and enhance existing API documentation; introduce a new template system for reusable scraping, crawling, and search configurations, including detailed examples and usage guidelines for template integration in API calls.

✨ feat(template): enhance template execution and validation by introducing a trusted flag for templates, allowing for improved security and flexibility in execution contexts; update database schemas and validation logic to support this feature, ensuring better handling of user-defined templates.

✨ feat(tests): introduce base Jest configuration for the AnyCrawl monorepo, enhancing ESM support, workspace package resolution, and coverage reporting; refactor existing package configurations to extend from the base config, ensuring consistency across all packages. Additionally, add new JSON schema validation tests and remove outdated test files for improved test coverage and clarity.

✨ feat(api): enhance template handling in Crawl, Scrape, and Search controllers by integrating template variables and improving request data merging; update schemas to support dynamic template variables for better flexibility in user-defined configurations.

✨ feat(api): enhance template handling by adding user permission checks for template access in Crawl, Scrape, and Search controllers; update TemplateHandler to validate user access based on template ownership and status.
✨ feat(api): enhance search route by adding locale mapping for unsupported languages, improving tokenizer language support in Orama.
✨ feat(docs): add MCP Server documentation and update middleware import path; enhance source configuration by removing unused attachFile reference.

readme_html:
📖 Overview

AnyCrawl is a high‑performance crawling and scraping toolkit:

  • SERP crawling: multiple search engines, batch‑friendly
  • Web scraping: single‑page content extraction
  • Site crawling: full‑site traversal and collection
  • High performance: multi‑threading / multi‑process
  • Batch tasks: reliable and efficient
  • AI extraction: LLM‑powered structured data (JSON) extraction from pages

LLM‑friendly. Easy to integrate and use.

🚀 Quick Start

📖 See full docs: Docs

Generate an API Key (self-host)

If you enable authentication (ANYCRAWL_API_AUTH_ENABLED=true), generate an API key:

pnpm --filter api key:generate
# optionally name the key
pnpm --filter api key:generate -- default

The command prints uuid, key and credits. Use the printed key as a Bearer token.

Run Inside Docker

If running AnyCrawl via Docker:

  • Docker Compose:
docker compose exec api pnpm --filter api key:generate
docker compose exec api pnpm --filter api key:generate -- default
  • Single container (replace <container_name_or_id>):
docker exec -it <container_name_or_id> pnpm --filter api key:generate
docker exec -it <container_name_or_id> pnpm --filter api key:generate -- default

📚 Usage Examples

💡 Use the Playground to test APIs and generate code in your preferred language.

If self‑hosting, replace https://api.anycrawl.dev with your own server URL.

Web Scraping (Scrape)

Example


curl -X POST https://api.anycrawl.dev/v1/scrape \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANYCRAWL_API_KEY' \
  -d '{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "engine": "cheerio"
}'

Parameters

Parameter Type Description Default
url string (required) The URL to be scraped. Must be a valid URL starting with http:// or https:// -
engine string Scraping engine to use. Options: cheerio (static HTML parsing, fastest), playwright (JavaScript rendering with modern engine), puppeteer (JavaScript rendering with Chrome) cheerio
proxy string Proxy URL for the request. Supports HTTP and SOCKS proxies. Format: http://[username]:[password]@proxy:port (none)
max_age number Cache control (ms). 0 = force refresh (skip cache read); > 0 = accept cached content within this age; omit to use default. (none)
store_in_cache boolean Cache control. Whether to store the result in cache. To bypass cache reads, use max_age=0. true

More parameters: see Request Parameters.

Cache details (self-host / S3 / map index): see docs/cache.md.

LLM Extraction

curl -X POST "https://api.anycrawl.dev/v1/scrape" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANYCRAWL_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "json_options": {
      "schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "company_mission": { "type": "string" },
          "is_open_source": { "type": "boolean" },
          "employee_count": { "type": "number" }
        },
        "required": ["company_mission"]
      }
    }
  }'

Site Crawling (Crawl)

Example


curl -X POST https://api.anycrawl.dev/v1/crawl \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANYCRAWL_API_KEY' \
  -d '{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "engine": "playwright",
  "max_depth": 2,
  "limit": 10,
  "strategy": "same-domain"
}'

Parameters

Parameter Type Description Default
url string (required) Starting URL to crawl -
engine string Crawling engine. Options: cheerio, playwright, puppeteer cheerio
max_depth number Max depth from the start URL 10
limit number Max number of pages to crawl 100
strategy enum Scope: all, same-domain, same-hostname, same-origin same-domain
include_paths array Only crawl paths matching these patterns (none)
exclude_paths array Skip paths matching these patterns (none)
scrape_options object Per-page scrape options (formats, timeout, json extraction, etc.), same as Scrape options (none)

More parameters and endpoints: see Request Parameters.

Search Engine Results (SERP)

Example

curl -X POST https://api.anycrawl.dev/v1/search \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANYCRAWL_API_KEY' \
  -d '{
  "query": "AnyCrawl",
  "limit": 10,
  "engine": "google",
  "lang": "all"
}'

Parameters

Parameter Type Description Default
query string (required) Search query to be executed -
engine string Search engine to use. Options: google google
pages integer Number of search result pages to retrieve 1
lang string Language code for search results (e.g., 'en', 'zh', 'all') en-US

Supported search engines

  • Google

❓ FAQ

  1. Can I use proxies? Yes. AnyCrawl ships with a high‑quality default proxy. You can also configure your own: set the proxy request parameter (per request) or ANYCRAWL_PROXY_URL (self‑hosting).
  2. How to handle JavaScript‑rendered pages? Use the Playwright or Puppeteer engines.
